root_text.sn35.148:1.3: katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, nibbānasappāyā paṭipadā?
root_text.sn35.148:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, cakkhuṁ dukkhanti passati, rūpā dukkhāti passati, cakkhuviññāṇaṁ dukkhanti passati, cakkhusamphasso dukkhoti passati, yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi dukkhanti passati …pe…
root_text.sn35.148:1.6: mano dukkhoti passati, dhammā dukkhāti passati, manoviññāṇaṁ dukkhanti passati, manosamphasso dukkhoti passati, yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi dukkhanti passati.
root_text.sn35.148:1.7: Ayaṁ kho sā, bhikkhave, nibbānasappāyā paṭipadā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.148:0.3: The Suffering as Conducive to Extinguishment
translation_text.sn35.148:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you a practice that’s conducive to extinguishment.
translation_text.sn35.148:1.3: And what is that practice that’s conducive to extinguishment?
translation_text.sn35.148:1.4: It’s when a mendicant sees that the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact are suffering. And they see that the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact is also suffering.
translation_text.sn35.148:1.5: They see that the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.148:1.6: mind, ideas, mind-consciousness, and mind contact are suffering. And they see that the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact is also suffering.
translation_text.sn35.148:1.7: This is that practice that’s conducive to extinguishment.”
